business.headline: Beyond the petrol pump: How higher fuel prices could hit India's economyThe Rs 3-per-litre petrol and diesel price hike may only be the start of a new cycle of fuel price increases as crude oil prices remain high, with economists warning of wider pressure on inflation, growth, household budgets and the rupee

Petrol, diesel prices hiked after years. Here's what else could get costlierRising petrol and diesel prices are starting to push up transport and household costs across India. Economists say the impact could spread to food, deliveries, air travel and inflation if crude stays high.
Sensex, Nifty open higher despite rise in fuel prices; IT stocks lead gainsThe S&P BSE Sensex was up 292.55 points to 75,691.27, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 83.70 points to 23,773.30 as of 9:34 am.

Fuel costlier by Rs 3 per litre: Why government raised petrol, diesel pricesThe government and state-run oil companies raised petrol and diesel prices by Rs 3 per litre on Friday after keeping rates unchanged for weeks.

8th Pay Commission: What happened at the Cabinet Secretary-led NCJCM meetingAt the NCJCM meeting on May 11, employee unions placed pay, pension, healthcare and staffing demands before senior government officials. The discussion gained added weight as it ran alongside 8th Pay Commission consultations and prompted directions on several pending issues.

India's fuel crisis could push a long-overdue rethink of how cities movePM Modi's seven appeals are the right signal at the right time. But fuel security and clean air require infrastructure, not just intention, and that demands a place in every state and central budget.

Petrol, diesel prices hiked by Rs 3 nationwide; CNG also up in Delhi by Rs 2 Live UpdatesIndia had so far avoided a major increase in retail fuel prices despite surging global crude oil prices as a result of the West Asian conflict. However, speculation over another round of hikes had been building for days as global crude continued to climb.

Govt caps duty-free gold imports at 100 kg under Advance Authorisation schemeGold imports in India, the world's second-biggest gold consumer after China, are driven by the jewellery industry demand. Such imports involve a substantial outflow of foreign exchange.

US moving to end $265 million fraud cases against Gautam Adani: ReportThe US Justice Department is reportedly set to drop criminal fraud and bribery charges against Gautam Adani linked to an alleged $265 million corruption scheme tied to India's largest solar power project, according to a Bloomberg report.

We asked top banks how they fight digital fraud. None respondedIn the third and final part of the series, Banking Risks in Digital India, IndiaToday.in reached out to major banks to understand how they are preparing for the next generation of digital fraud — from deepfakes and mule accounts to coercion-driven scams and AI-enabled deception. None of the banks responded.

Air India reports $2.8 billion loss in FY26, biggest since Tata takeoverAccording to Singapore Airlines' annual report, Air India's losses stood at 3.56 billion Singapore dollars during the financial year.

Paytm's pivotal year: How Vijay Shekhar Sharma steered Indian fintech's boldest turnaround storyPaytm reported its first full-year profit of Rs 552 crore in FY26, capping a sharp reset under Vijay Shekhar Sharma. The numbers shift attention from last year's regulatory shock to cost discipline, UPI growth and an AI-led strategy.

Your office commute is getting costlier. Is 'work from home' the answer?Getting to office is starting to feel expensive. With fuel price hike concerns growing and salaries already stretched by inflation and EMIs, employees are increasingly asking whether work from home deserves a second look.

China shows interest in US oil after Trump-Xi talks on Iran and Strait of HormuzAccording to a White House readout, Trump and Xi agreed that the Strait of Hormuz should be reopened to support global energy needs. The waterway has effectively remained closed since the start of the US and Israel conflict with Iran.

Sensex ends 790 points higher, Nifty above 23,600; IT stocks tumbleThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 789.74 points, or 1.06%, to close at 75,398.72, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 277 points, or 1.18%, to settle at 23,689.60.

8th Pay Commission: Why government employees want DA merged into basic payEmployee unions have requested the 8th Pay Commission to absorb Dearness Allowance (DA) into basic pay. The move could alter salary calculations, allowances, pensions and retirement benefits for lakhs of employees.

Bedridden, but asked to visit bank for re-KYC? Why this keeps happening in IndiaMany customers complain that despite digital infrastructure existing on paper, some branches still insist on physical presence, hard-copy paperwork, or repeated document submissions.

8th Pay Commission update: Employee body pushes for 50% guaranteed pension and moreAs discussions about the 8th Pay Commission continue, a key employee body has put forward a set of demands aimed at improving retirement security. At the centre of it is a proposal for an assured pension under the National Pension System (NPS).

India seeks extension of US waiver on Russian oil amid Iran conflict: ReportThe waiver, initially approved by the US in March and later expanded through an authorisation valid until May 16, allowed Indian refiners to continue receiving already-loaded Russian cargoes.

Why PM is asking Indians to cut spending: NITI Aayog explains austerity messageAs tensions in West Asia continue to rise, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged Indians to be careful with spending and avoid unnecessary expenses. But why now? Is this simply a precautionary step or a sign of deeper economic worries? NITI Aayog Vice Chairman Ashok Lahiri explains what the Prime Minister's austerity message really means.

entertainment.headline: Kartavya review: Saif Ali Khan shines in Netflix thriller that needed more rageRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026 Actor Saif Ali Khan's Kartavya turns a small-town murder probe into a study of caste, crime and duty. The film's ideas land, but its soft-edged execution blunts the force of its anger.

Sonu Sood slams trolling amid Alia Bhatt's Cannes appearance chatter Actor Sonu Sood has seemingly defended Alia Bhatt amid online chatter over her Cannes appearance. His cryptic message has sparked fresh discussion as fans and fellow actors rally behind Alia.

Beyond the tapori: Anil Kapoor's new life as Bollywood's hero in uniform Anil Kapoor moved away from his vintage tapori and romantic star days long ago. But he seems to be reinventing himself lately as a new-age hero in uniform, with projects such as 24, Subedaar, War 2 and Fighter.

Maa Behen: Madhuri Dixit, Triptii Dimri's film releases on Netflix in June Netflix has set June 4 as the premiere date for Maa Behen, starring Madhuri Dixit, Triptii Dimri, Dharna Durgaa and Ravi Kishan. The crime comedy follows a mother and her two daughters as they scramble to hide a dead body and protect their secret.

Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 streams uncut on Netflix US. Here's what is different Dhurandhar: The Revenge has begun streaming on Netflix internationally in a raw and uncut version with longer, more explicit scenes. The release has drawn attention online, with viewers comparing its restored violence and uncensored dialogue to the theatrical cut.

Rajinikanth's Jailer 2 crew member dies after electric shock on Chennai set A 28-year-old art department worker died after suffering an electric shock on the Jailer 2 set in Chennai. Police are investigating the suspected electrical leak, while the makers are yet to issue a statement.

Elon Musk says Christopher Nolan cast Lupita Nyong'o as Helen of Troy for awards Elon Musk has criticised Christopher Nolan's casting of Lupita Nyong'o as Helen of Troy in The Odyssey. His posts on X have amplified an online argument over race, beauty and interpretation in the film.

Rasika Dugal on missing mainstream offers: Happy to do song-and-dance number Actor Rasika Dugal said she never received real mainstream Hindi film offers, though she would have gladly done a song-and-dance film. She also expressed that women-led stories can draw audiences and that writing for female characters is improving.

Saif Ali Khan shares how fatherhood changed his view of long work shifts Actor Saif Ali Khan said fatherhood has made him more conscious of giving time to his family while discussing Kartavya. He said the film explores a dark moral conflict between conscience, duty and family.

Too long, too complex, too tiring: What went wrong with Splitsvilla X6? As MTV Splitsvilla X6 heads into its finale, the season's Pyaar Ya Paisa twist has drawn mixed fan reactions. Viewers say prolonged episodes and game-heavy turns diluted the romance that defines the show.

Suriya's Karuppu premieres with one-day delay, team apologises for release chaos Suriya's Karuppu released in theatres on May 15 after a one-day delay caused by financial issues. The postponement led to cancelled shows, apologies from the team and relief for waiting fans.

Who could be the next James Bond? Makers share casting update The hunt for the next James Bond has entered an active casting phase, with auditions now underway. The move signals real momentum on the next 007 film after years of speculation following Daniel Craig's exit.

Aakhri Sawal review: Sanjay Dutt's bold ideological film falters in dramatic excessRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026 Sanjay Dutt and Namashi Chakraborty deliver compelling face-offs in Aakhri Sawal that earnestly distinguish constructive discussion from adversarial debate and shines a light on RSS's unsung contributions. Is the film on opposing ideologies worth your time? Here is our review.

Diljit Dosanjh: Proud Punjabi, hero in India but quietly American He gave India "Main Hoon Punjab," India gave him the status of an icon. But between Coachella, Border 2 and global Punjabi pride, Diljit Dosanjh stopped being Indian one day.

Did Britney Spears bark, wave a knife at a restaurant? Singer's team reacts Britney Spears' representative denied reports that the singer behaved dangerously and walked around a Sherman Oaks restaurant with a knife during dinner. The rebuttal followed online claims about her alleged erratic behaviour.

Cannes 2026: Tara Sutaria channels timeless elegance at Women in Cinema gala Actor Tara Sutaria attended the Women in Cinema gala dinner at Cannes 2026 on Friday, where she was honoured for her contribution to cinema. Her ivory Vivienne Westwood look kept the focus on clean couture and marked a strong style moment at the festival.

It's Cannes, baby: Barbara Palvin, Dylan Sprouse reveal pregnancy on red carpet Supermodel Barbara Palvin and actor Dylan Sprouse revealed they are expecting their first child during their appearance at the Cannes Film Festival. The couple later confirmed the pregnancy on Instagram.

Jr NTR birthday surprise: NTRNeel teaser kept secret from team. Source Prashanth Neel will unveil the first glimpse of NTRNeel at midnight on May 20. As per sources, the teaser has been kept from Jr NTR and the film's team to avoid leaks and heighten curiosity.

My Royal Nemesis review: Lim Ji-yeon turns K-drama chaos into pure entertainmentRelease Date:Fri May 08, 2026 My Royal Nemesis is a wildly entertaining fantasy rom-com that throws every classic K-drama trope into one chaotic but addictive package. The Netflix drama turns that culture clash into brisk comedy, romance and questions about how history remembers women.

general news.headline: Breach in chain of command: Government takes responsibility for NEET leakSpeaking at a press conference on May 15, the Education Minister acknowledged a "breach in the chain of command" and said the government was taking responsibility for the NEET paper leak controversy.

Rajasthan family at centre of NEET leak row had obtained 2025 paper as well: SourcesInvestigators found that all five children of the Biwal family allegedly received the NEET 2025 paper before the exam, while Vikas Biwal, son of alleged kingpin Mangilal, is accused of helping circulate the leaked paper after Rs 65 lakh was allegedly paid to procure it.

Advisor to PM in race for Bengal Finance Minister's post, via NandigramEconomist Sanjeev Sanyal has emerged as the leading contender for Bengal's finance portfolio. The buzz has grown amid speculation that the Suvendu Adhikari-led government lacks a strong finance hand to steer Bengal's economy.

NEET-UG to go online next year. What changes for students?Following the disruption in NEET-UG 2026 due to reported irregularities, the government has announced key corrective steps, including a fresh exam date, fee waiver, and student-friendly measures. Alongside these immediate decisions, a major long-term shift towards computer-based testing from next year remains central to reforms.

The 1991 RBI secret-op that flew 47,000 kg of gold out of IndiaWhen India faced a balance-of-payments crisis in the late 1980s and early 1990s, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in 1991 airlifted nearly 47,000 kg of gold to England to raise a $405-million emergency loan and prevent a sovereign default. The shipment, carried out in secrecy and under tight security, was one of the defining moments that paved the way for India's economic reforms.

Live: PM on brief UAE visit amid Iran war, gets guard of honour on landingPM Modi Five Nation Tour Live Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is set to embark on a six-day visit to the United Arab Emirates (UAE), the Netherlands, Sweden, Norway and Italy beginning Friday, aimed at further strengthening India's bilateral ties amid ongoing geopolitical upheavals. Modi's first stop will be the UAE, where he is scheduled to meet President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan to advance the India-UAE Comprehensive Strategic Partnership and exchange views on the conflict in West Asia. The Prime Minister will visit the Netherlands from May 15 to 17, Sweden from May 17 to 18, Norway from May 18 to 19, and conclude his tour in Italy from May 19 to 20, where he will hold high-level talks with the leaders of the respective countries. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for all the latest updates.

Petrol, diesel prices hiked only by Rs 3. Why this could be the first of manyIndia has finally gone and increased the rate of petrol and diesel by Rs 3 a litre. That was bound to happen given the war in the Middle East and the supply disruptions. The hike is modest, according to experts, and there could be more coming. They suggest by how much more petrol and diesel prices can go up by.

Why has BCCI picked Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for India A and not India15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has earned his maiden India A call-up for the tour of Sri Lanka in June following his sensational performances for Bihar, India U-19 and the Rajasthan Royals in the Indian Premier League.

Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 streams uncut on Netflix US. Here's what is differentDhurandhar: The Revenge has begun streaming on Netflix internationally in a raw and uncut version with longer, more explicit scenes. The release has drawn attention online, with viewers comparing its restored violence and uncensored dialogue to the theatrical cut.

Live: Govt admits breach, June 21 re-exam set, AAP flags student panicNEET UG 2026 Re-Examination Live Updates: The May 3 NEET UG 2026 exam was cancelled to protect students' interests following paper leak allegations, Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan said, adding that the government remains committed to fighting the "exam mafia." He made the remarks during a national briefing on the controversy surrounding the exam. Meanwhile, the National Testing Agency (NTA) has announced a revised schedule, setting June 21 as the date for the re-examination. The decision comes after the earlier test was scrapped amid allegations of irregularities and an ongoing investigation. Over 22 lakh medical aspirants across the country will now have to appear for the fresh exam, which authorities have assured will be conducted under tighter security and improved transparency measures. Follow the latest updates on IndiaToday.in.

Man calls wife's lover home for drinks, then slits his throat in front of herAccording to officials, the incident took place on Wednesday when the accused, identified as Bhimraj Omprakash Sharma, invited Vikas Ashok Bhusare to his home. Sharma allegedly suspected Bhusare of having an affair with his wife.

NEET-UG retest on June 21, admit cards to be issued on June 14Education Minister Dharmendra Pardhan said that we accept the concerns of the students and have come up with the new date. Days after the NEET-UG 2026 examination was cancelled amid a major paper leak, the National Testing Agency has officially announced the new examination date. The re-exam will now be conducted on June 21 under heightened security and monitoring measures.

Shobhaa De's brutal Cannes commentary: Alia gets critique, Urvashi gets loveShobhaa De ripped into Indian celebrities' Cannes appearances in a blunt video commentary. Her remarks sharpened the debate over whether the festival is becoming more brand-driven than cinema-led.

Thailand Open 2026 live: Sindhu suffers quarters exit, Sat-Chi head to semisThailand Open 2026 Badminton Live Updates: PV Sindhu kept the fight going but Akane Yamaguchi somehow has squeezed in to take the second and third games to win the quarterfinal clash.

Xi says US is declining. Trump turns it into public post, redirects it to BidenTrump framed Xi's "declining America" remark as a criticism of the Biden era, while portraying his own presidency as a period of economic revival, military strength and renewed US global dominance during the ongoing Beijing summit.

Mega tsunami taller than Qutub Minar hit Alaska. No one knew it happenedScientists reconstructed a 481-metre tsunami that struck Alaska's Tracy Arm Fjord after a huge landslide. The near-miss event highlights how glacier retreat can destabilise slopes and trigger extreme waves.

When IIMs took to academic roasting with paper-leak questions after CAT cancellationMonths after the 2003 CAT question paper's leak forced the cancellation of the premier exam, the IIMs returned with a re-test featuring questions that mirrored the mechanics of the scandal itself. The now-legendary 'mastermind' CAT question is remembered by CAT aspirants as a rare moment which many call 'academic roasting'.

We set laws in Kerala: Muslim League raises provocative slogans after CM pickMuslim League's procession celebrating VD Satheesan's elevation as Kerala Chief Minister featured provocative slogans, also targeting caste organisation leaders.

These cities are at high risk from super El Nino set to hit IndiaAround 60% of Indian farmers are entirely dependent on monsoon rainfall for the kharif crop season, making the stakes for this year's rains exceptionally high.

China says will work with US as equals. Is Beijing more equal than others?China's repeated emphasis on 'equality, respect, and mutual benefit' signals that Beijing no longer wants to be treated as a rival to be contained, but as an equal power centre shaping the global order. The lavish welcome for Trump, amid the Iran war and deep strategic tensions, reflected Beijing's confidence that the world is no longer unipolar.

Satya Nadella reveals Microsoft's $25 billion plan to absorb OpenAIMicrosoft CEO Satya Nadella revealed in court that Microsoft prepared a $25 billion backup plan to absorb Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, and OpenAI employees after Altman's sudden firing in November 2023.

Preity Zinta, Ricky Ponting in tense chat after Punjab Kings crash in DharamsalaPunjab Kings co-owner Preity Zinta had a concerned look after the side lost their 5th game in a row in IPL 2026. In a nail-bitter, Mumbai Indians beat Shreyas Iyer's side by 6 wickets in a final-over thriller in Dharamsala.

sports.headline: Samson next India T20I captain? Shastri gives his seal of approval for CSK star Ravi Shastri has backed Sanju Samson as a serious candidate to lead India in T20Is. Shastri feels that Samson has answered his critics by showing consistency during the T20 World Cup and the ongoing IPL season.

I only listen to Rohit Sharma: Tilak Varma's heartfelt tribute after win vs PBKS Mumbai Indians star Tilak Varma paid an emotional on-field tribute to Rohit Sharma after defeating Punjab Kings. The youngster dedicated the win to the veteran opener, gesturing his jersey number 45 in Dharamsala.

MI batting coach Pollard cops hefty fine after outburst at umpire in PBKS clash PBKS vs MI: Mumbai Indians batting coach Kieron Pollard has been fined 15 per cent of his match fee for using abusive language towards the fourth umpire. The penalty came during a tense six-wicket victory over Punjab Kings in Dharamsala.

Why has BCCI picked Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for India A and not India 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has earned his maiden India A call-up for the tour of Sri Lanka in June following his sensational performances for Bihar, India U-19 and the Rajasthan Royals in the Indian Premier League.

Mumbai Indians players are happier when Hardik is not there: Kris Srikkanth Kris Srikkanth said the Mumbai Indians looked lighter and more united without Hardik Pandya in the dugout after the win over Punjab Kings. MI pulled off a stunning performance to defeat PBKS in Dharamsala.

Ban Arshdeep: L Sivaramakrishnan urges BCCI action for Tilak Varma racist remark Laxman Sivaramakrishnan has called for a season-long ban for Arshdeep Singh over a "racist" remark toward Tilak Varma. The veteran claims the BCCI has enough proof to act, stating that the modern game is a profession, not just a game.

Mbappe captain, Camavinga out as France announce FIFA World Cup 2026 squad Didier Deschamps has named France's 2026 World Cup squad with Kylian Mbappe as captain and Eduardo Camavinga omitted. It sharpens the focus on France's depth ahead of what is expected to be Deschamps' final World Cup in charge.

Sreejesh was insulted: Former captain Jude Felix tears into Hockey India Jude Felix has accused Hockey India of insulting PR Sreejesh by removing him from the junior men's team job. The former India captain also questioned the federation's decision to appoint Sreejesh and then not give him the required time in the role.

IPL Play of the Day: Captain Jasprit Bumrah, the friend at mid-off IPL 2026, PBKS vs MI: In an era built to break bowlers, Jasprit Bumrah strapped on the captain's armband for the first time in his IPL career and did something no stat line could capture: he became his bowlers' best friend.

Flat decks gone, Tilak Varma shows his class in Dharamsala Named the captain of India A for the tri-nation series, Tilak Varma proved the selectors right with a terrific knock against Punjab Kings. With pitches now slowing down, Tilak is showing that he is one of the class acts in the IPL.

Shardul Thakur addresses Hardik Pandya's prolonged absence amid trade rumoursIPL 2026: Mumbai Indians seamer Shardul Thakur has addressed captain Hardik Pandya's prolonged absence from the side. Shardul responded to the trade rumours that have caught fire on social media.

Tilak Varma scales new heights as Punjab's freefall in the mountains continuesIPL 2026, PBKS vs MI: Tilak Varma's blazing 75 lit up Dharamsala. Jasprit Bumrah marshals his troops with quiet authority on captaincy debut, and Punjab Kings find the mountain air thinner and colder with every passing defeat.

In a nail-bitter, Mumbai Indians beat Shreyas Iyer's side by 6 wickets in a final-over thriller in Dharamsala.

Shoddy PBKS lose 5 in a row. Can Shreyas Iyer's men qualify for playoffs?IPL 2026, PBKS vs MI: Punjab Kings have now lost five matches in a row and find themselves in a tricky position heading into their final two league games, with the race for the playoffs intensifying with every passing match.

Nikhat Zareen hits out at BFI-SAI tiff after crashing out of Asiad raceNikhat Zareen has hit out at the Boxing Federation of India and the Sports Authority of India after crashing out of the Asian Games and Commonwealth Games selection race. Nikhat said the last two weeks had been "torturous" for Indian boxers amid the ongoing tussle between the two bodies.

PR Sreejesh raises tough questions for Hockey India after controversial coaching exitPR Sreejesh has questioned Hockey India's coaching structure and growing reliance on foreign staff after his controversial exit from the junior men's setup despite a successful 18-month tenure.

Top government officers, bureaucrats enjoy sports fund meant for athletesAn investigation by The Indian Express has alleged that crores from the National Sports Development Fund were spent on sports infrastructure linked to bureaucrats and exclusive government institutions instead of athletes and public sporting projects.

Did not expect Test captaincy before Mumbai Indians role: Jasprit BumrahIPL 2026: Mumbai Indians handed Jasprit Bumrah the captaincy of the team for the first time in the Indian Premier League. At the toss, Bumrah joked that he did not expect to become the India Test captain before leading MI.

Jannik Sinner unstoppable, eclipses Novak Djokovic to set Masters world recordItalian Open 2026: World No.1 Jannik Sinner broke Novak Djokovic's record for the most consecutive wins in Masters 1000 events after defeating Andrey Rublev in the quarter-finals at Rome

Big blow for Nikhat Zareen: Boxing star set to miss out on Asian GamesNikhat Zareen lost 1-4 to Sakshi Chaudhary in the 51kg semi-finals at the Patiala trials on Thursday. The defeat rules the two-time world champion out of the Asian Games qualification.

Banter or racism? Arshdeep calls Tilak Varma 'oye andhere', faces backlashPunjab Kings fast bowler Arshdeep Singh has landed in trouble over his social media videos once again. In a video posted on Snapchat, Arshdeep can be heard calling Tilak 'andhera', which loosely translates to dark.

The Premier League Weekly: Dreams, doubts and drama in the final stretchArsenal's title hopes are alive again as Mikel Arteta's side regain momentum, but the Premier League drama stretches far beyond the summit. From Chelsea's managerial uncertainty to Manchester United's transfer dilemmas, the intrigue keeps building.

Drug ban setback to IPL resurgence: Kagiso Rabada recounts emotional 12 monthsAfter going through the lows of a drugs ban and the highs of winning an ICC title with South Africa, Kagiso Rabada said the difficult year taught him not to care too much about criticism and outside opinions.

PBKS vs MI Highlights: Punjab Kings lose 5 in a row, Mumbai Indians win by 6 wicketsPBKS (Punjab) vs MI (Mumbai) Highlights, IPL 2026: Mumbai Indians beat the Punjab Kings by 6 wickets in Dharamsala after chasing 201 in 19.5 overs. Tilak Varma's unbeaten 75 took Mumbai past the finish line.

Italian Open match halted as Inter Milan's fireworks celebration sweeps Rome arenaItalian Open 2026: The quarterfinal clash between Luciano Darderi and Rafael Jodar was temporarily halted after smoke from Inter Milan's Coppa Italia celebrations drifted into Rome's Foro Italico and disrupted visibility.

CSK sign South African all-rounder as replacement for injured Jamie OvertonChennai Super Kings have signed South African all-rounder Dian Forrester as a replacement for injured England all-rounder Jamie Overton for the remainder of the Indian Premier League 2026 season.

India won't play in FIFA World Cup 2026, but 3 Indian-origin players just mightIndia may not be on the plane to the 2026 World Cup, but the tricolour connection is far from broken. From a 19-year-old sensation from Kerala to a former Bayern Munich prodigy, three Indian-origin footballers could make history on the world stage. As global heavyweights finalise their squads, they discover the stars carrying the flag for the diaspora in North America.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in India colours as BCCI names squad for tri-seriesThe Indian Cricket Board has named the India A squad for tri-series against Sri Lanka A and Afghanistan A. Tilak Varma has been named as the captain of the 15-member squad.

Nahid Rana crowned ICC Player of the Month for stunning New Zealand displaysBangladesh pacer Nahid Rana won the ICC Men's Player of the Month award after spearheading Bangladesh's ODI series victory over New Zealand.

Golden chance for Arshdeep Singh to find form: S Badrinath ahead of MI clashS. Badrinath challenged Arshdeep Singh to rediscover his best against Mumbai Indians, dismissing fatigue concerns and calling the PBKS pacer's form crucial in the race for the IPL playoffs.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
